LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JULIAN BIGGS' MOTION TO DISMISS. THE MOTION IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFFS DID NOT SERVE THE COMPLAINT ON DEFENDANT WITHIN THREE YEARS OF FILING THE ACTION. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO SHOW THAT THE SERVICE AT 301 MISSION ST. ON FEBRUARY 28, 2013, WAS PROPER. THE EVIDENCE INDICATES THAT THE ADDRESS WAS NOT DEFENDANT'S RESIDENCE OR PLACE OF WORK WITHIN THE MEANING OF CCP 415.20. DISMISSAL OF THE ACTION IS MANDATORY UNDER CCP 583.210(A) AND 583.250. PLAINTIFFS FAIL TO PROVIDE A BASIS FOR TOLLING THE 3-YEAR PERIOD PURSUANT TO CCP 583.240. PLAINTIFFS' ARGUMENT THAT THE TIME PERIOD WAS TOLLED BECAUSE THEY EXERCISED REASONABLE DILIGENCE FAILS. IN BARRINGTON V. A.H. ROBINS CO. (1985) 39 CAL.3D 146, THE COURT EXPLAINS THAT THE LEGISLATURE ABROGATED THAT LINE OF AUTHORITY. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR# 5976

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-23-2014 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-01-2014 Continued To Jul-09-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Moiton And Moiton To Dismiss
Off Calendar Apr-23-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Julian Biggs, Tel. 415.370.9370, defendant and moving party appearing on his own behalf;
Jason Lundberg, Esq., Allegiance Law, Tel. 415.404.6395, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. After argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT JULIAN BIGGS' Motion To Dismiss. The motion is granted. Plaintiffs did not serve the complaint on defendant within three years of filing the action. Plaintiff fails to show that the service at 301 Mission St. on February 28, 2013, was proper. The evidence indicates that the address was not defendant's residence or place of work within the meaning of CCP 415.20. Dismissal of the action is mandatory under CCP 583.210(a) and 583.250. Plaintiffs fail to provide a basis for tolling the 3-year period pursuant to CCP 583.240. Plaintiffs' argument that the time period was tolled because they exercised reasonable diligence fails. In Barrington v. A.H. Robins Co. (1985) 39 Cal.3d 146, the court explains that the Legislature abrogated that line of authority. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: A. James Robertson; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976

Notice Of Moiton And Moiton To Dismiss
Off Calendar Apr-17-2014 Continued To Apr-23-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT JULIAN BIGGS' Motion To Dismiss is continued to April 23, 2014 to give moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the reply papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than April 17, 2014 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the moving and opposition papers. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =(302/EHG)
